title: Making candles out of beef tallow
description: During the wet years (1893-5), her aunt made candles out of beef tallow for the Christmas tree and the church.

title: Christmas Eve celebrations and walking to Christmas day service
description: Christmas eve the gala day of the year for Scandinavians. Walking to early church service Christmas Day at 5 a.m.

title: Food they ate at Christmas dinner and mother making lots of sweets
description: Food eaten at Christmas dinner. Mother made ten gallons of cookies and fancy candies.

title: What kind of gifts the grandparents brought; father having only 5 dollars in 1893 and brother distributing gifts
description: Grandparents brought gifts, candy oranges and two coconuts. In 1893 Father had only $5 for Christmas. Older brother would distribute gifts around the Christmas tree.

title: Jewish owners of David's were well accepted; playing with African-Americans; Chinese-Americans had several laundries
description: David's originally owned by Kaufman and Durnam. Well accepted even though Jewish. Everyone played with Mary McCrae, a Black girl, it didn't make any difference. Chinese had several laundries.

title: Father worked in Colorado mines; homesteaded near Pasco
description: Father worked in Leadville, Colorado, mines. Homesteaded in Hog Heaven near Pasco, and left his equipment buried in the sand.

title: Father working with the railroad from Spokane to Juliaetta; father walking from Juliaetta to Moscow to keep a date with her mother
description: Father and his crew excavated railroad bed from Spokane to Juliaetta using mules and scrapers. He walked from Juliaetta to Moscow to keep a date with her mother.

title: Mrs. Chapman's first husband is killed by Native Americans
description: Mrs. Chapman's first husband killed by Indians in the very early years. She grabs the lines and drives home.

title: Frequently saw Native Americans near home; brothers steal father's dynamite
description: Frequently saw Indians near home. Brothers steal father's dynamite.

title: Dipped grain for seed in Blue Vitriol; early laundries
description: Dipped grain for seed in Blue Vitriol. Early laundries

title: Mixing up clothes starch for the clothes; Washing clothes
description: Mixing up clothes starch for the clothes. Washing clothes.

title: Father threshing business; father didn't trust banks and being paid in gold
description: Father's thrashing operation continued after it snowed. In the early years paid in gold - farmers didn't believe in banks.
